					<div id="mw-content-text" class="mw-body-content"><div class="mw-subjectpageheader">
</div><div class="mw-content-ltr mw-parser-output" lang="en" dir="ltr"><p class="mw-empty-elt"> 

</p><p>On 26 June 1964, <a href="/wiki/Swiss_Post" title="Swiss Post">Swiss Post</a> introduced postal codes as the third country after Germany (1941) and the United States (1963).
</p><p>In Switzerland, the postal codes have four digits. As with the postcode system introduced in Germany in 1993, a municipality can receive several postcodes. A locality (settlement) having its own postal code does not mean that it is an independent political municipality, but only that it is an official locality. In addition, a postcode can include several political communes (e.g.: 3048 <a href="/wiki/Worblaufen" class="mw-redirect" title="Worblaufen">Worblaufen</a>, includes parts of the communes of <a href="/wiki/Bern" title="Bern">Bern</a> and <a href="/wiki/Ittigen" title="Ittigen">Ittigen</a>) or several cantons (e.g.: 8866 <a href="/wiki/Ziegelbr%C3%BCcke" title="Ziegelbrücke">Ziegelbrücke</a>, includes parts of the <a href="/wiki/Cantons_of_Switzerland" title="Cantons of Switzerland">cantons</a> of <a href="/wiki/Canton_of_Glarus" title="Canton of Glarus">Glarus</a> and <a href="/wiki/Canton_of_St._Gallen" title="Canton of St. Gallen">St. Gallen</a>), which is why it is not possible to assign it unambiguously in both directions. In addition, it is often not possible to assign a unique postcode to post offices (post boxes) in larger cities. For this reason, six-digit postcodes are used internally.<sup class="noprint Inline-Template Template-Fact" style="white-space:nowrap;">[<i><a href="/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed" title="Wikipedia:Citation needed"><span title="This claim needs references to reliable sources. (July 2019)">citation needed</span></a></i>]</sup>
</p><p>The <a href="/wiki/Principality_of_Liechtenstein" class="mw-redirect" title="Principality of Liechtenstein">Principality of Liechtenstein</a> is also included in the Swiss postal code system, as is the German enclave of <a href="/wiki/B%C3%BCsingen_am_Hochrhein" title="Büsingen am Hochrhein">Büsingen am Hochrhein</a>, which has its own Swiss postal codes in addition to its national one, DE-78266. Before January 2020, the Italian enclave of <a href="/wiki/Campione_d%27Italia" title="Campione d'Italia">Campione d'Italia</a> also had a Swiss postcode, CH-6911, but this ceased to be valid, and all mail requires the use of the Italian postcode IT-22061.<sup id="cite_ref-1" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-1"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>1<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> This followed the enclave's entry into the <a href="/wiki/European_Union" title="European Union">European Union</a>'s <a href="/wiki/European_Union_Customs_Union" title="European Union Customs Union">Customs Area</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-2" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-2"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>2<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> Also in Italy, <a href="/wiki/Swiss_Post" title="Swiss Post">Swiss Post</a> previously held an office in <a href="/wiki/Domodossola" title="Domodossola">Domodossola</a> with the code CH-3907.<sup id="cite_ref-3" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-3"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>3<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup> This is now used for the village of <a href="/wiki/Simplon,_Valais" title="Simplon, Valais">Simplon</a>.<sup id="cite_ref-4" class="reference"><a href="#cite_note-4"><span class="cite-bracket">[</span>4<span class="cite-bracket">]</span></a></sup>
</p>
<meta property="mw:PageProp/toc">
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Format_of_postal_codes_(PLZ/NPA)"><span id="Format_of_postal_codes_.28PLZ.2FNPA.29"></span>Format of postal codes (PLZ/NPA)</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Postal_codes_in_Switzerland_and_Liechtenstein&action=edit&section=1" title="Edit section: Format of postal codes (PLZ/NPA)"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<p>The Swiss postal codes are assigned geographically, from west to east. They do not follow political divisions (cantons, districts), but they follow a <i>routing</i> allocation, following railways and <a href="/wiki/PostBus_Switzerland" title="PostBus Switzerland">PostBus</a> routes. The postal code of big cities finish with 00, and it is not allocated if in the region there is not a big center.<sup class="noprint Inline-Template Template-Fact" style="white-space:nowrap;">[<i><a href="/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed" title="Wikipedia:Citation needed"><span title="This claim needs references to reliable sources. (July 2019)">citation needed</span></a></i>]</sup>
</p><p><a href="/wiki/Switzerland" title="Switzerland">Switzerland</a> is divided into nine postal districts, numbered from west to east. Each district is subdivided into postal areas.
Each area contains a maximum of one hundred units.
</p><p>The postal codes are made up as follows:
</p>
<dl><dd>3436 Zollbrück</dd>
<dd>3 = district (Bern)</dd>
<dd>34 = area (Burgdorf)</dd>
<dd>343 = route (Burgdorf - Langnau)</dd>
<dd>3436 = post office number (Zollbrück)</dd></dl>
<p>Today, the third digit has no real meaning anymore. In the past, mail was assigned to fixed railway or truck routes, but modern logistics do not need this practice any more.
</p><p>Postal codes of <a href="/wiki/Liechtenstein" title="Liechtenstein">Liechtenstein</a> are included in the same structure, using the range from 9480 to 9499.
</p>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Summary_of_postal_codes">Summary of postal codes</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Postal_codes_in_Switzerland_and_Liechtenstein&action=edit&section=2" title="Edit section: Summary of postal codes"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>

<li>85xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Frauenfeld" title="Frauenfeld">Frauenfeld</a>, <a href="/wiki/Weinfelden" title="Weinfelden">Weinfelden</a>, <a href="/wiki/Amriswil" title="Amriswil">Amriswil</a>, <a href="/wiki/Romanshorn" title="Romanshorn">Romanshorn</a></li>
<li>86xx - Region <a href="/wiki/D%C3%BCbendorf" title="Dübendorf">Dübendorf</a>, Zürcher Oberland, <a href="/w/index.php?title=Seebezirk_(St._Gallen)&action=edit&redlink=1" class="new" title="Seebezirk (St. Gallen) (page does not exist)">See</a> (SG)</li>
<li>87xx - Region Right shore of <a href="/wiki/Lake_Zurich" title="Lake Zurich">Lake Zurich</a>, <a href="/wiki/Gaster_(district)" class="mw-redirect" title="Gaster (district)">Gaster</a>/See (SG), <a href="/wiki/Canton_Glarus" class="mw-redirect" title="Canton Glarus">Canton Glarus</a></li>
<li>88xx - Region Linkes Zürichseeufer, <a href="/wiki/Ausserschwyz" class="mw-redirect" title="Ausserschwyz">Ausserschwyz</a> (<a href="/wiki/March_(district)" class="mw-redirect" title="March (district)">March</a>, <a href="/wiki/H%C3%B6fe_(district)" class="mw-redirect" title="Höfe (district)">Höfe</a>, <a href="/wiki/Einsiedeln_SZ" class="mw-redirect" title="Einsiedeln SZ">Einsiedeln SZ</a>), <a href="/w/index.php?title=Glarner_Unterland&action=edit&redlink=1" class="new" title="Glarner Unterland (page does not exist)">Glarner Unterland</a>, <a href="/wiki/Sarganserland" title="Sarganserland">Sarganserland</a>/<a href="/wiki/Lake_Walen" class="mw-redirect" title="Lake Walen">Lake Walen</a></li>
<li>89xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Limmattal" class="mw-redirect" title="Limmattal">Limmattal</a>, <a href="/wiki/Albis" title="Albis">Albis</a>, <a href="/wiki/Knonauer_Amt" class="mw-redirect" title="Knonauer Amt">Knonauer Amt</a>, <a href="/wiki/Mutschellen" title="Mutschellen">Mutschellen</a> and <a href="/w/index.php?title=Kelleramt&action=edit&redlink=1" class="new" title="Kelleramt (page does not exist)">Kelleramt</a> (easternmost <a href="/wiki/Aargau" title="Aargau">Aargau</a>)</li></ul></li>
<li>9xxx - Region Eastern Switzerland (<a href="/wiki/Ostschweiz" class="mw-redirect" title="Ostschweiz">Ostschweiz</a>)
<ul><li>90xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Canton_of_St._Gallen" title="Canton of St. Gallen">St. Gallen</a>, <a href="/wiki/Appenzell_(town)" class="mw-redirect" title="Appenzell (town)">Appenzell</a></li>
<li>91xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Herisau" title="Herisau">Herisau</a></li>
<li>92xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Gossau_SG" class="mw-redirect" title="Gossau SG">Gossau</a>, <a href="/wiki/Flawil" title="Flawil">Flawil</a>, <a href="/wiki/Uzwil" title="Uzwil">Uzwil</a>, <a href="/wiki/Bischofszell" title="Bischofszell">Bischofszell</a></li>
<li>93xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Arbon" title="Arbon">Arbon</a></li>
<li>94xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Rorschach_(Wahlkreis)" title="Rorschach (Wahlkreis)">Rorschach</a>, <a href="/w/index.php?title=Rheintal_(Alpenrhein)&action=edit&redlink=1" class="new" title="Rheintal (Alpenrhein) (page does not exist)">Rheintal</a>, <a href="/wiki/Liechtenstein" title="Liechtenstein">Liechtenstein</a></li>
<li>95xx - Region <a href="/wiki/Wil" title="Wil">Wil</a></li>
<li>96xx - <a href="/wiki/Toggenburg" title="Toggenburg">Toggenburg</a></li></ul></li></ul>
<div class="mw-heading mw-heading2"><h2 id="Cities">Cities</h2><span class="mw-editsection"><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">[</span><a href="/w/index.php?title=Postal_codes_in_Switzerland_and_Liechtenstein&action=edit&section=3" title="Edit section: Cities"><span>edit</span></a><span class="mw-editsection-bracket">]</span></span></div>
<ul><li>1211: <a href="/wiki/Geneva" title="Geneva">Geneva</a> (generic post code for all <i>P.O. Box</i> addresses in Geneva; the address is followed by a number indicating the exact post office in which the box is located, e.g. <i>1211 Geneva 7</i>: 1 Mont-Blanc, 10 United Nations, 11 Rue du Stand, 12 <a href="/wiki/Champel" title="Champel">Champel</a>, 13 Les Charmilles, 14 State Hospital, 16 Grand-Pré, 17 Malagnou, 18 St-Jean, 19 Petit-Saconnex, 2 Cornavin/Swisscom, 20 CIC, 21 Les Pâquis, 22 International Labour Organization, 23 CERN, 24 <a href="/wiki/Les_Acacias" title="Les Acacias">Les Acacias</a>, 26 La Praille/Military base, 27 World Health Organization, 28 Le Bouchet, 3 Rive, 4 Plainpalais, 5 Main Post Office, 6 Les Eaux-Vives, 7 Servette, 70 CS, 8 Jonction, 84 Voting system, 9 La Cluse)</li>
<li>80xx: The city districts of Zurich were numbered before the Swiss postal codes were introduced; the number of the city district equals the last numbers of the postal code. The administration of the <a href="/wiki/Canton_of_Zurich" title="Canton of Zurich">canton of Zurich</a> has the postal code 8090.</li>
<li>3003 is the postal code of the Federal administration which is located in Bern.</li></ul>
